findings.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Drops to 4.29% on U.S.-Iran Ceasefire
findings.summary: U.S. 10-year Treasury yield fell to 4.29% as easing geopolitical tensions from U.S.-Iran ceasefire boosted demand for government bonds globally. This reflects investor reassessment of central bank policy expectations amid lower risk premiums, supporting bond prices in a risk-on shift.
findings.claim_excerpt: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield declined to around 4.29%, while the 2-year yield closed near 3.79%.
findings.signal_type_basis: Episodic geopolitical relief driving yield moves, likely noise unless sustained 3+ weeks.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Temporary bond rally from de-escalation; monitors for persistence amid tariff regime.
findings.confidence_basis: Direct market data from established financial source with consistent reporting.

sources_cited.excerpt: 10-year Treasury yield at 4.29% on ceasefire news.
Bond yields fell, with the 10-year Treasury yield at 4.30%.

search_queries_run: bond yields US Treasury past 48 hours
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Multiple excerpts from same high-quality source confirm yield level and trigger.

findings.review_reason: Assess if yield decline sustains beyond geopolitical noise.

findings.title: WTI Crude Plunges 15% to $96 on U.S.-Iran Ceasefire
findings.summary: Oil prices dropped sharply with WTI crude falling ~15% to $96/bbl following U.S.-Iran ceasefire easing supply fears. This cyclical relief pressures energy sector equities and input costs, though reacceleration risks noted from prior elevations.
findings.claim_excerpt: Oil prices were sharply lower, with WTI crude down roughly 15% and closing around $96 per barrel.
findings.signal_type_basis: Event-driven price swing from ceasefire, not sustained trend.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Short-term energy disinflation; watch for rebound if tensions resume.
findings.confidence_basis: Precise price data corroborated across market recap entries.

sources_cited.excerpt: Easing geopolitical tensions weighed on oil prices.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Consistent reporting on magnitude and cause from reliable source.

cross_monitor_relevance.erm: Lower energy costs ease AI capex pressures short-term.

findings.review_reason: Monitor for oil price stabilization or reversal.

findings.title: Core PCE Eases to 3.0%, Housing Inflation at 3.1%
findings.summary: Core PCE inflation dipped to 3.0% with housing at 3.1%, aiding services moderation but still above Fed's 2% target. Expectations of reacceleration from oil pass-through temper bond market optimism.
findings.claim_excerpt: Core PCE edged down to 3.0%; housing inflation eased to 3.1%.
findings.signal_type_basis: Medium-term inflation trend moderation influencing rate expectations over weeks.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Supports Fed easing path but vulnerable to commodity rebounds.
findings.confidence_basis: Specific data points from timely market analysis.

sources_cited.excerpt: Inflation remains elevated with Fed easing.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Cross-source alignment on elevated but easing inflation.

findings.title: AI Infrastructure Capex Bolsters GDP Amid Tariff Volatility
findings.summary: Significant AI-infrastructure capital outlays supported 1.8% GDP growth despite Liberation Day tariff shocks and shutdown. This structural tech investment offsets trade uncertainty, sustaining expansion into 2026.
findings.claim_excerpt: Resilient consumer spending and significant AI-infrastructure capital outlays supported GDP growth.
findings.signal_type_basis: Regime-level tech capex trend playing out over years despite macro shocks.
findings.preliminary_assessment: AI capex as key growth driver in tariff-constrained environment.
findings.confidence_basis: Major bank's macro outlook with historical context.

sources_cited.excerpt: AI-infrastructure capital outlays supported trends like GDP growth.
Markets close higher amid ongoing volatility.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: JPM outlook corroborated by market resilience reports.

cross_monitor_relevance.erm: Core AI capex signal reinforcing energy demand long-term.

findings.review_reason: Quantify capex flows vs. tariff drag.

findings.title: $6T Global Market Wipeout Tied to Tariff Uncertainty
findings.summary: Global markets lost over $6T in 48 hours with stocks, oil, gold, crypto falling amid tariff policy signals. This highlights prolonged tension forcing central bank liquidity responses over time.
findings.claim_excerpt: In two days, over six trillion dollars vanished from global markets.
findings.signal_type_basis: Tariff-driven uncertainty as medium-term trend per protocol upgrade.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Risk-off embedded in tariff regime; not new tightening.
findings.confidence_basis: Single source but aligns with tariff cascade expectations.

sources_cited.excerpt: $6 Trillion Erased in 48 Hours from tariff announcements.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: One source; needs corroboration but fits protocol.

findings.title: Fed Resumes Easing with 50bp Cuts Amid Labor Softness
findings.summary: Fed implemented 50bp insurance cuts this fall due to labor moderation, ending QT Dec 1, with more expected early 2026 targeting 3.25-3.50%. This cyclical support counters tariff-induced uncertainty.
findings.claim_excerpt: Fed cut 50bp this fall, another in early 2026 to 3.25-3.50%.
findings.signal_type_basis: Medium-term policy response to data over weeks/months.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Easing cycle offsets tariff slowdown risks.

sources_cited.excerpt: 50bp insurance cuts by Fed this fall.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: JPM details supported by yield reactions.

findings.review_reason: Monitor data dependency amid shutdown data gaps.

findings.title: Dollar Falls 1% on Ceasefire Risk-On Shift
findings.summary: USD weakened nearly 1% vs. developed currencies as U.S.-Iran ceasefire spurred risk-on flows. Tariff false-positive discipline applies: not a new tightening signal.
findings.claim_excerpt: Easing geopolitical tensions weighed on the dollar, down nearly 1%.
findings.signal_type_basis: Episodic currency move from geopolitics, per false-positive rules.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Temporary USD relief; embedded in tariff risk-off baseline.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Geopolitical trigger corroborated by macro context.

findings.review_reason: Apply false-positive check vs. tariff baseline.
